name: Greek Mythology
description: The gods, heroes and monsters that shaped the myths of ancient Greece.

group.definition: King of the gods and lord of the sky and thunder, ruling the other **Olympians** from **Mount Olympus**.
Wife and sister of **Zeus**, queen of the gods and goddess of marriage; she cast **Hephaestus** from Olympus.
God of the sea and earthquakes, brother of **Zeus** and **Hades**, wielding a three-pronged trident.
Goddess of wisdom, warfare and craft, and patron of Athens; she lent **Perseus** a reflective shield against **Medusa**.
God of the sun, music, prophecy and healing, and twin brother of **Artemis**.
Goddess of the hunt, wild animals and the moon, daughter of **Zeus** and twin of **Apollo**.
Goddess of love, beauty and desire, born from sea foam and wed to **Hephaestus**.
Messenger of the gods, wearing winged sandals; he guides souls toward the **Underworld**.
God of war and violent conflict, son of **Zeus** and **Hera**.
God of fire, blacksmiths and craftsmanship; cast from Olympus by **Hera** for his deformity.
God of wine, theatre and religious ecstasy, born from the thigh of **Zeus** after his mother Semele died.
Brother of **Zeus** and ruler of the **Underworld**, the realm of the dead that bears his name.
Wife of **Hades** and queen of the **Underworld**; eating pomegranate seeds binds her to return there each winter.
The ferryman who rows the dead across the river **Styx** in exchange for a coin.
The personification of death, once tricked and chained by **Sisyphus** so that no mortal could die.
The river bordering the **Underworld**; **Achilles** was dipped in it to become nearly invulnerable.
The Titan who stole fire from Olympus for humanity and was chained to a rock by **Zeus** as punishment.
The Titan condemned by **Zeus** to hold up the heavens for eternity after the Titanomachy.
Goddess of witchcraft, magic, crossroads and ghosts, an ancient chthonic deity of the night.
The Three Fates who weave every mortal's destiny; **Atropos** cuts the thread of life to end it.
Hero who performed twelve labours as penance, including slaying the **Lernean Hydra**.
Hero who beheaded **Medusa** by watching her reflection in a shield from **Athena**.
Hero who entered the **Labyrinth** of Crete and killed the **Minotaur**.
Near-invincible hero of the Trojan War, vulnerable only at the heel by which his mother dipped him in the **Styx**.
Hero who slew the fire-breathing Chimera with the aid of the winged horse **Pegasus**.
Captain of the Argonauts who led the quest aboard the Argo to retrieve the Golden Fleece.
A Gorgon with snakes for hair whose gaze turns onlookers to stone; slain by **Perseus**.
A creature with the head of a bull and body of a man, kept in the **Labyrinth** and killed by **Theseus**.
The winged horse born from the blood of **Medusa**, later ridden by **Bellerophon**.
A multi-headed serpent that grew two heads for each one cut off, defeated by **Heracles** as his second labour.
The multi-headed hound that guards the gates of the **Underworld** ruled by **Hades**.
The highest mountain in Greece and home of the **Olympians**, ruled by **Zeus**.
The subterranean realm of the dead ruled by **Hades**, reached by crossing the river **Styx**.
The maze on Crete designed by Daedalus to contain the **Minotaur**.
The jar opened by Pandora, the first mortal woman, releasing all evils into the human world.
